title: 2022 Leeds Rhinos season

{{Use dmy dates|date=September 2022}} {{Use British English|date=September 2022}} {{Infobox rugby league team season | year = 2022 | team_colour = blue; border: solid gold 2px | font_colour = white; | league = Super League | league_link = [[Super League XXVII|Super League]] | league_rank = 5 | league_wins = 14 | league_draws = 1 | league_losses = 12 | points_for = 577 | points_against = 528 | playoff_result = | cup = Challenge Cup | cup_result = Sixth Round | cup_wins = 0 | cup_losses = 1 | CEO_title = Chairman | CEO = {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Paul Caddick]] | coach_title = Head Coach | coach = [[Richard Agar]] (until March 2022)
[[Jamie Jones-Buchanan]] (interim)
[[Rohan Smith (rugby league)|Rohan Smith]] (from April 2022) | captain = [[Kruise Leeming]] & [[Ash Handley]] | stadium = [[Headingley Rugby Stadium|Headingley]]
[[Leeds]], [[Yorkshire|United Kingdom]] | agg_attendance = | avg_attendance = 12,382 | low_attendance = | high_attendance = 15,418 (v {{leagueicon|Castleford|12}} [[Castleford Tigers]]) | top_tries = {{flagicon|ENG}} [[Ash Handley]] (17) | top_goals = {{flagicon|PNG}} [[Rhyse Martin]] (84) | top_points = {{flagicon|PNG}} [[Rhyse Martin]] (212) | prev_season = [[2021 Leeds Rhinos season|2021]] | next_season = [[2023 Leeds Rhinos season|2023]] | list = yes | list_link = List of Leeds Rhinos seasons }} This article details the [[Leeds Rhinos]]'s rugby league football club's 2022 season. They were coached by [[Richard Agar]] until May 2022, [[Jamie Jones-Buchanan]] as an interim coach and [[Rohan Smith (rugby league)|Rohan Smith]] from April 2022 onwards. The Rhinos competed in both the [[Super League XXVII|2022 Betfred Super League]] and the [[2022 Challenge Cup]]. ==Season review== On 12 February, Leeds began their Super League campaign with a 22–20 loss to [[Warrington Wolves]]{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36608/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-1 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 1 |publisher=RFL |date=13 February 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} followed by losses at [[Wigan Warriors]]{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36653/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-2 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 2 |publisher=RFL |date=20 February 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} and at home to [[Catalans Dragons]]{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36688/betfred-super-league-round-up |title=Betfred Super League round-up|publisher=RFL |date=28 February 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} before their first win came in Round 4 with a 34–18 victory at [[Wakefield Trinity]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36745/betfred-super-league-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=6 March 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} Leeds had an early exit from the [[Challenge Cup]] with a 40–16 defeat by [[Castleford Tigers]],{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/36891/betfred-challenge-cup-round-6-round-up |title=Betfred Challenge Cup Round 6 Round-Up |publisher=RFL |date=27 March 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} and their run of poor results leading to the departure of [[Richard Agar]] as head coach meant that by mid-April Leeds were 11th in the table.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60978/saints-make-history-by-completing-four-peat |title=Saints make history by completing four-peat |publisher=RFL |date=24 September 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37169/betfred-super-league-round-11-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 11 round-up |publisher=RFL |date=1 May 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} Interim head coach [[Jamie Jones-Buchanan]] oversaw wins against [[Toulouse Olympique]] and [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] before the arrival of the newly appointed [[Rohan Smith (rugby league)|Rohan Smith]].{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/37111/betfred-super-league-round-10-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round 10 round-up |publisher=RFL |date=24 April 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} Towards the end of the season, Leeds went on a run of six wins{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60813/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-25|title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 25 |publisher=RFL |date=26 August 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} before suffering a 32–18 loss to Catalans Dragons at the end of August. It meant that to qualify for the play-offs Leeds needed to avoid defeat in the final round of the regular season against Castleford, which they achieved with two late tries that gave them a 14–8 victory.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60825/betfred-super-league-round-up |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}}publisher=RFL |date=30 August 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}}{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60859/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-round-27 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} Round 27 |publisher=RFL |date=3 September 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} Leeds began their play-off campaign by travelling to Perpignan where a hat-trick by [[Liam Sutcliffe]] gave them a 20–10 win over Catalans Dragons.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60894/betfred-super-league-round-up-%7C-september-10 |title=Betfred Super League Round-Up {{!}} September 10 |publisher=RFL |date=10 September 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} Leeds faced Wigan in the semi-final. At half time the score was 4–2 to Wigan, but a try from [[Jarrod O'Connor]] (his first for Leeds) and two from [[James Bentley (rugby league)|James Bentley]] saw Leeds reach their 11th [[Super League Grand Final|Grand Final]] with a 20–8 win.{{cite web|url=https://www.rugby-league.com/article/60938/leeds-and-st-helens-to-meet-in-betfred-super-league-grand-final |title=Leeds and St Helens to meet in Betfred Super League Grand Final |publisher=RFL |date=18 September 2022 |access-date=16 February 2024}} The Grand Final took place on 24 September at [[Old Trafford]] where Leeds lost 24–12 to [[St Helens R.F.C.|St Helens]]. ==Results== {{clear}} ===Pre-season friendlies=== {{legend2|#CFC|Win|border=1px solid #000000|text=W}} {{legend2|#FFE3E3|Loss|border=1px solid #000000|text=L}} {{table
